title: They Say That 'Time Assuages,
content:
They say that 'time assuages,' --
    Time never did assuage;
    An actual suffering strengthens,
    As sinews do, with age.

    Time is a test of trouble,
    But not a remedy.
    If such it prove, it prove too
    There was no malady.
meaning:
"They Say That 'Time Assuages," is a quintessential example of Emily Elizabeth Dickinson's signature style...

### Why We Love This Line
At Linespedia, we believe that poetry is the ultimate sanctuary for the soul...
